heroSubtitle: New to AI Tokens? Don't rush to model comparisons, pricing tables, or APIs just yet. This guide walks you through the concepts, calculation logic, and cost structure in the right order — so you can make confident decisions about what to read next.
stepsTitle: If You're New, Read in This Order

children.text: First time encountering AI Tokens? You don't need to rush into model comparisons, pricing tables, or APIs. This page will walk you through the concepts, calculation logic, and cost structure in the simplest sequence — then help you decide what to read next.

readingSteps.title: Understand What an AI Token Actually Is

children.text: Build the most basic concept first: an AI Token is not points, not an API key — it's the
unit of computation
a model uses when processing input and generating output.

readingSteps.linkLabel: Read: What is an AI Token →

readingSteps.title: Learn How AI Tokens Are Calculated

children.text: Once you know what a Token is, the next step is understanding the relationship between
input, output, word count, and usage
. This is where the numbers start to make sense.

readingSteps.linkLabel: Read: How AI Tokens Are Calculated →

readingSteps.title: Understand How Costs Are Derived

children.text: Once you understand how usage is formed,
pricing, costs, and platform differences
will click into place much more easily. Don't start here — it'll make more sense after steps 1 and 2.

readingSteps.linkLabel: Read: How AI Token Pricing Works →

children.text: Most beginners stumble not because of the tools — but because of
the order they approach things in
. The most common mistake is jumping straight to model comparisons, pricing tables, or platform plans before understanding the underlying concepts.

If you haven't first understood what tokens are, how input and output work, and how costs are formed — reading more data just creates more confusion. For most beginners, building a solid foundation first is more valuable than rushing to compare which model is cheapest.

Once you know the basic terminology, the next step is understanding the relationship between input, output, and usage. Just knowing that longer input and more output generally means higher usage makes everything else easier to absorb.
Once you have a basic grasp of concepts and calculation, looking at pricing, costs, and model differences becomes much less overwhelming than starting with a wall of numbers.

stuckCallouts.title: Then look at pricing and models

children.text: With a clear conceptual foundation in place, you can start exploring different models' cost differences and real-world use cases in depth — and actually understand what you're reading.

faq.question: Do I need to understand APIs first?

children.text: No. If you just want to understand what AI Tokens are, how they're calculated, and how costs work — you don't need to know how to write code first. Most beginners can start by building the basic concepts alone. Once you're ready to actually connect a model to a website, tool, or workflow, that's when the API content becomes relevant.

faq.question: Are AI Tokens the same as points or credits?

children.text: Not exactly. AI Tokens are closer to the computational units a model uses when processing text — input and output combined. Points and credits are display formats different platforms use to represent usage. Some platforms convert tokens to points, others show raw usage directly. The names vary across platforms, but the underlying logic isn't always the same.

faq.question: Why does my usage go up even when I haven't asked much?

children.text: Because AI Token usage isn't measured by how many times you asked — it's measured by how long each input was and how long the output is. If you paste in a long document, ask for a detailed response, or accumulate a lot of context in one conversation, usage can grow faster than you'd expect. It adds up across both what you send and what the model sends back.

faq.question: Does a higher price always mean a better model?

children.text: Not necessarily. Pricing is related to model capability, speed, context length, stability, and brand positioning — but not every use case needs the most expensive model. Some scenarios need fast response, stable cost, and "good enough" quality more than they need top-tier specs. Higher price reflects more capability, not necessarily more value for your specific task.
Once you've built up the basic concepts, these are the best articles to read next — they'll help you connect token counts, input/output differences, and usage patterns into a complete picture.

ctaTitle: Get the foundation right — the rest follows
ctaBody: First time with AI Tokens? You don't need to read every model comparison and pricing table upfront. Build the basic concepts, calculation logic, and cost understanding first — then the model comparisons, use cases, and platform differences will be much easier to navigate.

seo.seoTitle: AI Token Beginners Guide — Start Here
seo.seoDescription: New to AI Tokens? Learn what they are, how they're calculated, and how costs work — in the right order. The clearest beginner's guide to AI tokens.
